headlines.title: Scott Mills questioned by police in 2018 over sexual offence allegations against teenage boy
headlines.description: The case was dropped in 2019 after the CPS deemed there was insufficient evidence to bring charges, police say.

headlines.title: Starmer gives doctors 48 hours to cancel strike or lose new jobs package
headlines.description: The prime minister says the NHS could lose 1,000 extra training places if resident doctors go ahead with a six-day strike next week.

headlines.title: Hundreds of my abusers are still out there, says victim as grooming inquiry launched
headlines.description: There needs to be accountability for the failings that led to this mass exploitation of children in UK, says survivor.

headlines.title: New bin rules begin in England but not all councils are ready
headlines.description: Weekly food waste collections are required for all English homes as new legislation takes effect.

headlines.title: 'Something wasn't right': Wrong sperm given to UK families by IVF clinics in northern Cyprus
headlines.description: Families of seven children believe the wrong sperm or egg donors were used in their IVF treatment.

headlines.title: Why the benefit used by over 8 million people may not be fit for the future
headlines.description: Can Universal Credit and the work and benefits system more generally reshape itself to meet a new reality?

headlines.title: Three charged with murder over death of girl, 16
headlines.description: Three people are due to appear in court over the death of Chloe Watson Dransfield in Leeds.

headlines.title: Palestinians convicted of deadly attacks face death penalty under new Israeli law
headlines.description: The new law, passed on Monday, was pushed hard by the far-right and Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir.

headlines.title: Housing market to soften amid Iran war fallout, Nationwide says
headlines.description: The lender says the market regained momentum in March, but rising mortgage and energy costs could hit consumer confidence.

headlines.title: 'I sent eight letters': Drivers hope for payout from car finance redress scheme
headlines.description: Millions of motorists could be entitled to compensation with the financial regulator setting out how to apply

headlines.title: Eurovision Song Contest launches first-ever Asia edition
headlines.description: Broadcasters from 10 countries, including South Korea and the Philippines, will be taking part.

headlines.title: Inside the cockpit of RAF tanker during defensive mission against Iranian drones
headlines.description: Defence correspondent Jonathan Beale flies onboard a Royal Air Force Voyager as it refuels jets in the Middle East.

headlines.title: How Pakistan won over Trump to become an unlikely mediator in the Iran war
headlines.description: Pakistan's role as intermediary in this conflict took many by surprise. But perhaps it shouldn't have.

headlines.title: What was the 1970s oil crisis, and are we heading for something worse?
headlines.description: While both crises involve oil, experts say there are some important differences between what happened in the 1970s and today.

headlines.title: How the US could try to seize Iran's Kharg Island
headlines.description: Donald Trump has indicated he could send troops to take control of Iran's key oil export terminal.

headlines.title: Long queues in Myanmar as Iran war fuel crisis deepens
headlines.description: Drivers are queuing for hours at petrol stations in Myanmar as the Iran war continues to send shockwaves across the globe.

headlines.title: The personal items the Artemis II crew are taking to the Moon
headlines.description: The crew, who are accomplished pilots, engineers and scientists, reveal their hopes, fears and family sacrifices.

headlines.title: Huge fires at Russian oil facilities following Ukraine strikes, satellite images show
headlines.description: BBC Verify examines recent damage to three key Russian oil export facilities near the Baltic Sea.

headlines.title: 'Fake landlord lied that his dad had died to scam my deposit'
headlines.description: The Met Police says it is investigating a number of suspected frauds in relation to flat rental.

headlines.title: How will car finance compensation payments work?
headlines.description: Compensation could be given to many of those who took out a car loan between April 2007 and November 2024.

headlines.title: The Papers: 'Scott Mills sacked by BBC' and 'Starmer's 48-hour ultimatum'
headlines.description: Former BBC presenter Scott Mills appears on most of the papers, after he was sacked following allegations about his personal conduct.

headlines.title: How Scotland selected their base camp for this summer's World Cup
headlines.description: Gary Dicker and Steven Naismith explain why Scotland picked Charlotte FC as their World Cup base.

headlines.title: Shock, sadness and relief in town at centre of Australia's seven-month police manhunt
headlines.description: Porepunkah has been in the spotlight since Dezi Freeman shot dead two police officers last year.

headlines.title: Government to overhaul non-crime hate incident rules
headlines.description: The Home Office says updated guidance will end the policing of "everyday arguments" online.

headlines.title: Veteran abused for being gay seeks £50k MoD payout
headlines.description: A reparations scheme offers up to £70k to those who were forced to leave the military for being gay.

headlines.title: Sexual violence part of 'everyday life' in parts of Sudan, charity says
headlines.description: Victims describe how they have been targeted while carrying out everyday activities.

headlines.title: Kid Rock sparks US Army probe after helicopter flyby at his mansion
headlines.description: The country rock artist posted footage on Saturday of himself saluting the aircraft from his outdoor pool.

headlines.title: Congressman writes to King Charles urging him to meet Epstein victims during US visit
headlines.description: While the trip is not officially confirmed, the King and Queen Camilla are reportedly planning to visit the US at the end of April.

headlines.title: 'A succulent Chinese meal' - iconic Australian quote immortalised in national film archive
headlines.description: The theatrical monologue given by Jack Karlson as he was arrested in 1991 has been officially recognised as a "lasting piece of cultural folklore".

headlines.title: After sex abuse claims, activists and lawmakers rethink Cesar Chavez Day
headlines.description: California has renamed Cesar Chavez Day to Farmworkers Day after the labour-rights icon was accused of sexual abuse.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Scott Mills Sacked By BBC
headlines.description: Scott Mills has been sacked by the BBC following allegations about his personal conduct.

headlines.title: What is RFK doing to vaccines for children in America?
headlines.description: Trump’s Health Secretary hits a major setback to “Make America Healthy Again”

headlines.title: Newcastle not looking to make Howe change at moment - CEO
headlines.description: Newcastle United CEO David Hopkinson says he does not have a stance on the future of head coach Eddie Howe, but the club are "not looking to make a change at the moment".

headlines.title: Is Verstappen's threat to quit a lever to alter rules? - F1 Q&A
headlines.description: BBC Sport F1 correspondent Andrew Benson answers your questions after the Japanese Grand Prix.

headlines.title: Arsenal's 11 international withdrawals - injury crisis or crafty caution?
headlines.description: With 11 Arsenal players withdrawing from international duty, are the Gunners facing an injury crisis or are they simply being cautious?

headlines.title: How Scotland found their base camp for 'travelling' World Cup
headlines.description: Gary Dicker and Steven Naismith explain why Scotland picked Charlotte FC as their World Cup base.

headlines.title: Why innovative Canadian league is trialling daylight offside rule
headlines.description: BBC Sport looks at why the Canadian Premier League is trialling Arsene Wenger's 'daylight' offside rule in 2026.

headlines.title: Wales v Northern Ireland - the match nobody wants
headlines.description: Wales host Northern Ireland in a friendly in Cardiff on Tuesday because both teams lost their World Cup play-off semi-finals on Thursday.

headlines.title: Ranking the 10 most exciting wingers in the world
headlines.description: Few things in football are more exciting than an in-form wide man, but who are the most exciting in the world right night?
